completion_criteria_checked: docs/BRR_POLICY.md gains an 'Out-of-Scope Detection' section defining all three named failure modes checkably. -- MET: read the full added section directly; each of the three modes has a concrete, mechanically-applicable check, not a vague description.

The section defines an explicit, enumerated truth-surface list or clear rule. -- MET: an enumerated list of specific docs plus 'everything under schemas/' and 'everything under scripts/' is present, with an explicit note on how it grows (existing New Canonical Doc Process, not a new rule).

The section requires a structural verification procedure recorded via the existing out_of_scope_findings field, no new schema field. -- MET: a five-step procedure is stated; confirmed via check-schemas.ps1 (still PASS) that no schema was touched.

The section stays structural/measurable per BRR_PLAN.md Phase 3's special caution. -- MET: the section explicitly disclaims semantic/intent detection and limits itself to enumerable, diff-checkable criteria.

The section does not alter the self-verification fallback, autonomous gate, Acceptance Boundary Rules, or Task Safety Class core meaning. -- MET: re-read DECISION-033/036's full text and confirmed byte-for-byte unchanged via git diff (grep for removed/altered lines referencing DECISION-033/036 returned zero matches); confirmed scripts/check-autonomous-gate.ps1 and scripts/check-stop-conditions.ps1 are untouched (not in the file-changed list); Task Safety Classification and Acceptance Boundary Rules sections re-read, unchanged.

The section does not broaden into a larger governance redesign. -- MET: scope is confined to detection criteria and a verification procedure; no new verdict, authority, or governance mechanism is introduced.
A new decision is recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md. -- MET: DECISION-048 present, correctly cross-referenced, also records the owner's bootstrap/circularity-boundary confirmation from this cycle's chat.
No runtime script, schema, or enforcement change is introduced. -- MET: git diff confirms no file under scripts/ or schemas/ changed.

No existing verdict, task safety class, or stop condition is redefined or weakened. -- MET: the section reuses the existing OUT_OF_SCOPE verdict and out_of_scope_findings field only; spot-checked against Owner Review Matrix row 7, no redefinition found.

out_of_scope_findings: Applying this task's own new Out-of-Scope Detection procedure to itself: (1) all changed files enumerated via git diff --stat -- docs/BRR_POLICY.md, docs/DECISIONS.md, task-state.json, project-state.json, .cockpit/handoff/*, .cockpit/result/*; (2) every file checked against boundaries.allowed -- all covered; (3) truth-surface check -- docs/BRR_POLICY.md and docs/DECISIONS.md are truth surfaces but were both specifically named in this task's boundaries.allowed, so not a finding; no file under schemas/ or scripts/ was touched; (4) silent adjacent-scope check -- the docs/BRR_POLICY.md diff is confined to one new appended section matching the objective, and the docs/DECISIONS.md diff is confined to one new appended decision; no edits to unrelated existing content in either file. (5) Recorded here as required: none found.
risks: Self-verified under DECISION-033 degraded fallback (Codex unavailable). No independent second-party (Codex) review occurred. GPT secondary review of this specific completed result: not performed this cycle.

Verification Depth Policy row applied: Class B, truth-surface-affecting -> strict. Strict depth was applied (full read of the added section plus line-by-line cross-check against Owner Review Matrix row 7, Task Safety Classification, Acceptance Boundary Rules, Verification Depth Policy, and Self-Verification Restrictions, performed and recorded here, not merely asserted).

Circularity-restriction self-check: this task does NOT fall into the circularity case defined in pcc-brr3-002 -- it does not modify DECISION-033/036's text, the autonomous gate scripts, the Acceptance Boundary Rules, or a Task Safety Class's core definition; it adds a new adjacent detection-criteria section. It was therefore correctly eligible for self-verification under the existing rule, not an exception to it.

This is the second consecutive self-promoted task. Unlike pcc-brr3-002 (GPT-suggested, independently re-verified), this cycle had the owner's direct, explicit pre-authorization to self-promote this specific item plus two stop-conditions -- both held (confirmed above), but the underlying scoping choices (what belongs on the truth-surface list; where 'silent adjacent-scope edit' starts) remain PCC's own drafting judgment and are worth independent re-check.

The truth-surface list is a snapshot; if a new canonical doc is added later without this list being updated, detection of edits to that new doc as 'truth-surface' would silently under-fire until the list is refreshed. Disclosed in the policy text itself, not hidden.

The procedure is definitional/manual only -- nothing automates it; its real-world value depends on a verifier (self or independent) actually running the five steps each cycle, same enforcement gap disclosed for the two prior Phase 3 sections.

DECISION-036's commit-and-push-every-PASS authorization remains lapsed in BRR Phase 3. Per the owner's explicit instruction this cycle, pcc-brr3-002's commit (281781e) and this task's commit are to be pushed only if/when separately instructed -- neither is pushed as part of this close-out.
summary: Verified pcc-brr3-003 (BRR Policy: Out-of-Scope Detection) at 'strict' depth. Read the full added 'Out-of-Scope Detection' section directly, cross-checked it against Owner Review Matrix row 7, Task Safety Classification, Acceptance Boundary Rules, the Verification Depth Policy, and the Self-Verification Restrictions, and confirmed via git diff that DECISION-033/036's text is byte-for-byte unchanged and no scripts/schemas were touched. Applied the task's own new detection procedure to itself as an extra check: no out-of-scope findings. All nine completion criteria are met with direct evidence. Independent local guardrails clean. This is the second consecutive self-promoted task, this time under the owner's direct, explicit pre-authorization (plus two stop-conditions, both confirmed held) rather than GPT's suggestion -- recorded accurately in promotion_basis and DECISION-048 as corroborating context, with the independent gate check as the actual basis.
next_action: Task 'pcc-brr3-003' is complete and verified PASS. Close out and commit locally per the standing DECISION-020 default. Do not push pcc-brr3-002's commit (281781e), this commit, or any future commit to any remote without a fresh, explicit owner instruction each time. Owner/advisor decides whether to self-promote BRR Phase 3 item 4 (Inadequate-Work Return Path) next, direct it explicitly, or pause here -- this cycle does not pre-decide that.
